query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Enamor', 'To attract, to capture', 'The author hoped the new policy would enamor tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Encamp', 'To build and/or inhabit a camp or temporary shelter', 'The author hoped the new policy would encamp tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Encapsulate', 'To cover something as if in a capsule', 'Over time, the community learned to encapsulate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Encipher', 'Convert (a message or piece of text) into a coded form; encrypt', 'Her encipher became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enclave', 'A country, or part thereof, that is surrounded by another', 'Scholars noted a striking enclave between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Encomium', 'A piece of writing that praises someone or something highly', 'Her encomium became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Encompass', 'To include or surround', '“The park encompasses several hiking trails and lakes.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Encore', 'To call for an extra performance or repetition', 'Critics argued that the plan might encore the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Encourage', 'To mentally support, to motivate, give courage', 'The author hoped the new policy would encourage tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Encroach', 'To advance gradually and in a way that causes damage', 'The author hoped the new policy would encroach tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Encumber', 'To load down something with a burden', 'Critics argued that the plan might encumber the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Encumbrance', 'Something that encumbers, a burden that must be carried', 'Scholars noted a striking encumbrance between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Encyclical', 'Intended for general circulation', 'The essay highlights the encyclical of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Encyclopedia', 'A book or set of books containing articles on various topics', 'The essay highlights the encyclopedia of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Endanger', 'To expose to danger, to risk causing harm to', 'Critics argued that the plan might endanger the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Endear', 'To make dear or precious', 'The author hoped the new policy would endear tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Endeavor', 'A serious attempt or effort', '“She embarked on an endeavor to write a novel.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Endemic', 'Natural to or characteristic of a specific people or place', 'Scholars noted a striking endemic between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Endorse', 'To support or give one''s approval to, especially officially', 'Critics argued that the plan might endorse the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Endow', 'To furnish, as with some talent, faculty, or quality', 'The essay highlights the endow of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Endue', 'To invest or endow with some gift, quality, or faculty', 'The essay highlights the endue of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Endur', 'Lasting, permanent, present participle of endure', 'Her endur became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Endurance', 'The measure of a person''s stamina or persistence', 'The essay highlights the endurance of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Endure', 'To hold out against, sustain without impairment or yielding', 'The essay highlights the endure of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Energetic', 'Possessing, exerting, or displaying energy', 'The essay highlights the energetic of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enervate', 'To weaken or drain energy', 'Over time, the community learned to enervate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enfeeble', 'To make feeble, to weaken', 'Over time, the community learned to enfeeble its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enfranchise', 'To admit to citizenship, especially to grant voting rights', 'The author hoped the new policy would enfranchise tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Engage', 'To occupy the attention or efforts', 'Scholars noted a striking engage between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Engender', 'To cause or give rise to a particular feeling, situation, or condition', 'The author hoped the new policy would engender tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Engrave', 'To carve text or symbols into (something)', 'The author hoped the new policy would engrave tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Engross', 'To occupy completely, as the mind or attention', 'The essay highlights the engross of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Engulf', 'To overwhelm', 'Over time, the community learned to engulf its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enhance', 'To improve or make better', '“This software enhances image quality.”', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enigma', 'A person or thing that is mysterious', 'Her enigma became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enigmatic', 'Difficult to interpret or understand', 'What seemed enigmatic at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enjoin', 'To lay upon, as an order or command, to order, to charge', 'The author hoped the new policy would enjoin tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enkindle', 'To kindle, to arouse or evoke', 'The author hoped the new policy would enkindle tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enlighten', 'To supply with light, to illuminate', 'Critics argued that the plan might enlighten the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enlist', 'To join a cause or organization, especially military', 'Critics argued that the plan might enlist the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enmity', 'A feeling or condition of hostility, hatred, animosity', 'The essay highlights the enmity of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ennoble', 'To bestow with nobility, honour or grace', 'Critics argued that the plan might ennoble the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ennui', 'Listlessness from lack of excitement', 'The essay highlights the ennui of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enormity', 'Greatness of size, scope, extent, or influence, immensity', 'Scholars noted a striking enormity between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enormous', 'Deviating from the norm, unusual, extraordinary', 'The reviewer described the tone as enormous, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enormousness', 'Great size or magnitude', 'The essay highlights the enormousness of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enrage', 'To fill with rage, to provoke to frenzy or madness', 'Critics argued that the plan might enrage the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enrapture', 'To fill with great delight or joy, to fascinate', 'The author hoped the new policy would enrapture tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ensconce', 'To place in a secure environment', 'Critics argued that the plan might ensconce the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enshrine', 'To enclose (a sacred relic etc ) in a shrine or chest', 'Over time, the community learned to enshrine its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
